Learnscrollarchitecture · v0 · MIT

The shape of the codebase — optimised for fork, self-host, and donate.

An open-source product lives or dies by how readable its repo is. Every choice below favours simplicity, single static binaries, and zero vendor lock — so a stranger can git clone, docker compose up, and run their own LearnScroll in fifteen minutes.

01The stack

Mobile
React Native (Expo SDK 51)Brief's pick. EAS for builds. Single iOS+Android codebase.
Web
Next.js 15 PWAApp Router. SSR for /card/[id] OG images — the share growth lever.
Shared UI
packages/ui (TS)Card components shared between mobile and web. NativeWind or Tamagui for cross-platform styling.
API
Go + chi + sqlcSingle static binary, ~10MB RAM idle. Type-safe SQL without ORM tax.
Database
PostgreSQL 16 + pgvectorCards, users, progress, embeddings. One Postgres, no microservice DB sprawl.
Cache
RedisPer-user feed buffer (next 20). Rate limits. Session cache.
Queue
River (Postgres-backed)No new infra. Reuses your DB. Enrichment + moderation jobs.
AI jobs
Claude Haiku via APIHook rewriter, "Explain this", language tutor, moderation.
Auth
Magic link + Passkey (cookie sessions)No JWT. No vendor. Trivial to revoke. Self-host friendly.
SR
FSRS-rs (or pure-Go port)Modern replacement for SM-2. Used by current Anki.
Storage
Cloudflare R2Audio (~15KB/file). Zero egress — critical for free product.
CDN
CloudflareFree tier covers all card text + images.
Deploy
Docker Compose + Hetzner$10/$65/$220 VPS tiers. Caddy auto-HTTPS in front.
Monorepo
pnpm workspaces + TurborepoStandard, fork-friendly. Go services live alongside in /services.
CI
GitHub ActionsFree for public repos. Lints YAML community cards on every PR.
Telemetry
OpenTelemetry (optional)No vendor baked in. Forkers can wire to whatever they like.

02Repository layout

A single pnpm + Go workspace. apps/ is what users see, services/ is what runs in production, packages/ is shared TS, db/ is the source of truth, content/ is the community's home.

▸learnscroll/
▸apps/— User-facing apps. Each is independently deployable.
▸mobile/— React Native (Expo SDK 51). iOS + Android.
·app//— Expo Router file-based routing.
·components//— Mobile-only chrome (gestures, native sheets).
·app.json— Expo config.
·eas.json— EAS Build profiles (dev, preview, production).
·package.json
▸web/— Next.js 15 App Router PWA. SSR for shareable /card/[id] OG images.
·app//— Next.js routes — feed, topics, progress, profile, /card/[id].
·app/card/[id]/opengraph-image.tsx— Generates the share image at request time. THE growth lever.
·public/manifest.json— PWA manifest. Installable on any device.
·service-worker.ts— Offline buffer of next 20 cards.
·package.json
▸services/— Backend Go services. Each is a single static binary.
▸api/— REST + WebSocket public API. The only service exposed to the internet.
·cmd/api/main.go— Entrypoint. Wires deps, starts chi router on :8080.
·internal/handlers/feed.go— GET /v1/feed, /v1/feed/stream (WS).
·internal/handlers/cards.go— GET/POST /v1/cards, react, bookmark.
·internal/handlers/progress.go— GET /v1/progress, POST /v1/progress/sync.
·internal/handlers/threads.go— GET /v1/threads/:id (rabbit holes).
·internal/handlers/auth.go— Email magic-link + passkey.
·internal/ranker/feed.go— Weighted scoring. Topic affinity + type history + SR boost + recency. NO LLM.
·internal/ranker/feed_test.go— Snapshot tests on synthetic histories.
·internal/db//— sqlc-generated query types. 100% type-safe SQL.
·go.mod
▸enrich/— Batch worker. Runs Claude Haiku enrichment + moderation via River queue.
·cmd/enrich/main.go— Worker pool entrypoint.
·internal/jobs/hook_rewriter.go— Few-shot prompt + before/after fixtures per topic.
·internal/jobs/quiz_distractors.go— Generates 2 distractors for fact→quiz conversion.
·internal/jobs/related_cards.go— Embedding-based; pgvector search for nearest 3.
·internal/jobs/moderation.go— Approve / flag / reject community submissions.
·fixtures/hooks//— Curated before/after pairs. Edit these to tune voice.
▸ingest/— CLI tools that turn raw sources into our card schema.
·cmd/lsctl/main.go— Single binary: `lsctl ingest <source> <path>`.
·internal/sources/anki.go— Reads .apkg (zip → SQLite). Maps to vocab/quiz cards.
·internal/sources/tatoeba.go— TSV bulk parse. Sentence cards.
·internal/sources/wiki.go— MediaWiki API. Lead paragraphs → fact/concept.
·internal/sources/openstax.go— OpenStax REST. Chapter Q&A → quiz cards.
·internal/normalize/schema.go— Single output type. All sources land here.
▸packages/— Shared TypeScript code. Imported by both apps.
·core//— Domain types (Card, User, Progress), API client, FSRS port, mix algorithm.
·ui//— Card components (Fact, Vocab, Sentence, Quiz, Concept). Shared between mobile + web.
·icons//— Inline SVG diagram set (octopus, silk-road, cell, etc.). Programmatic only — no rasters.
·config//— tsconfig, eslint, prettier presets.
▸db/— PostgreSQL schema, migrations, seed data. Source of truth.
·migrations//— goose-style numbered SQL files. Forward-only.
·queries//— sqlc input. Each .sql becomes Go functions.
·sqlc.yaml
·seed//— Dev fixtures — 200 cards, 5 users.
▸content/— Community contributions. Plain YAML — easy to PR.
·community/biology//
·community/history//
·community/japanese//
·CONTRIBUTING.md— Card schema, voice guide, examples of strong vs weak hooks.
·schema.json— JSON Schema for card YAML. Used by the CI linter.
▸deploy/— Self-hosting reference. The most-read folder by forkers.
·docker-compose.yml— One-command local + prod. Postgres + Redis + api + enrich.
·Dockerfile.api— Multi-stage. Final image ~25MB.
·Dockerfile.enrich
·caddy/Caddyfile— Reverse proxy + auto-HTTPS via Let's Encrypt.
·hetzner//— Reference Terraform for the $10/$65/$220 VPS tiers.
·SELFHOST.md— 15-minute self-host guide. Tested every release.
▸.github/— CI: lint, test, build, lint community YAML, run moderation on PRs.
·workflows/test.yml
·workflows/build.yml
·workflows/content.yml— Validates community/**.yaml against schema.
·README.md— What it is, screenshots, 1-line install, link to SELFHOST.
·LICENSE— MIT.
·pnpm-workspace.yaml
·turbo.json

03API surface

Versioned at /v1. WebSocket only for the live feed; everything else is plain REST + JSON. Auth is cookie sessions, not JWTs (simpler to revoke, simpler to self-host).

Feed
GET/v1/feed?cursor=&types=Returns next 20 cards. Mix rule applied server-side. Cursor is opaque.
WS/v1/feed/streamStreams ranked cards as user scrolls. Backpressure-aware.
GET/v1/threads/:idRabbit hole sub-feed. 5–8 related cards.
Cards
GET/v1/cards/:idSingle card hydrate. SSR-friendly for share pages.
POST/v1/cards/:id/reactBody: { kind: love|blown|knew|bookmark|deeper }. Idempotent.
POST/v1/cards/:id/quizBody: { picked: int }. Records answer for SR weighting.
Progress
GET/v1/progressStreak, daily goal, topic bars, due-review queue.
POST/v1/progress/syncBatch: client uploads N events. Brief §architecture.
GET/v1/progress/dueSpaced-repetition due cards. Local-first; this is the catch-up sync.
Lang
POST/v1/vocab/knownMark word known. Updates i+1 model.
GET/v1/vocab/knownReturns user's known-word set.
AI
POST/v1/ai/explainCard-scoped chat. 3–4 sentence cap server-enforced.
POST/v1/ai/tutorLanguage mode. Sentence gen, correction, i+1 surfacing.
Share
POST/v1/share/previewReturns signed URL to a generated PNG (9:16, 1:1, 16:9).
Auth
POST/v1/auth/magicEmail magic link.
POST/v1/auth/passkeyWebAuthn registration / login.
Submit
POST/v1/community/submitUser card submission → enqueues moderation.

04Content pipeline

Raw sources → lsctl ingest → moderation worker → cards table. Community PRs land as YAML in /content/ and flow through the same pipeline.

01 · Source
Raw input
Anki .apkg, Tatoeba TSV, Wikipedia API, OpenStax JSON, community YAML.
02 · Ingest
lsctl CLI
One Go binary, one parser per source. Outputs the unified card schema.
03 · Enrich
Haiku batch
Hook rewrite, distractors, related-card linking, difficulty tag, embedding.
04 · Moderate
AI + human
Auto: facts, sources, dupes. Flagged → human queue. Rejected → reason logged.
05 · Serve
api → feed
Ranker pulls from cards + card_progress, applies mix rule, streams to client.

05Database schema

Five core tables. card_progress holds FSRS state per (user, card). feed_events is the append-only log the ranker reads.

-- learnscroll/db/migrations/001_init.sql

CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

CREATE TYPE card_type AS ENUM ('fact','vocab','sentence','quiz','concept');

CREATE TABLE cards (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  type            card_type NOT NULL,
  language        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'en',
  front           TEXT NOT NULL,
  back            TEXT,
  context         TEXT,
  source_url      TEXT,
  source_name     TEXT,
  tags            TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  difficulty      SMALLINT CHECK (difficulty BETWEEN 1 AND 5),
  audio_url       TEXT,
  diagram_kind    TEXT,
  related_ids     UUID[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  quiz_options    JSONB,
  quiz_answer_idx SMALLINT,
  embedding       vector(1024),
  approved        BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX cards_tags_gin   ON cards USING GIN (tags);
CREATE INDEX cards_front_trgm ON cards USING GIN (front gin_trgm_ops);
CREATE INDEX cards_emb_hnsw   ON cards USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops);

CREATE TABLE users (
  id             UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  email          CITEXT UNIQUE,
  handle         TEXT UNIQUE,
  language_goal  TEXT,
  daily_target   SMALLINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 10,
  mix_ratio      JSONB,
  streak_count   INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  streak_paused  BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  shield_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE card_progress (
  user_id      UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  card_id      UUID REFERENCES cards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  stability    REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  difficulty   REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  due_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
  last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  reps         INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  lapses       INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  reaction     TEXT,
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, card_id)
);
CREATE INDEX cp_due ON card_progress (user_id, due_at) WHERE due_at IS NOT NULL;

CREATE TABLE known_words (
  user_id   UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  language  TEXT NOT NULL,
  lemma     TEXT NOT NULL,
  marked_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, language, lemma)
);

CREATE TABLE feed_events (
  id         BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id    UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  card_id    UUID REFERENCES cards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  kind       TEXT NOT NULL,
  payload    JSONB,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX fe_user_recent ON feed_events (user_id, created_at DESC);

CREATE TABLE submissions (
  id         UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  author_id  UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  payload    JSONB NOT NULL,
  status     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  reason     TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

06Hosting tiers

Targets from the brief, mapped to Hetzner SKUs. Donations cover infra at every tier with room to spare.

0–1k users
$10
Single Hetzner CX22 (4GB). Postgres + Redis + api + enrich on one box. Caddy reverse proxy.
1k–20k users
$65
CX42 (16GB) for db+app. R2 bucket for audio. Cloudflare in front.
20k–100k users
$220
Split: db box (CX42), app box (CX32 ×2 behind LB), Redis on db box. Read replicas optional.

07Non-negotiables

No vendor lock.Postgres, Redis, R2 (S3-compatible), Cloudflare. All swappable. No Supabase / Firebase / Auth0.
One-binary services.Go static binaries. docker compose up is the whole prod stack.
Local-first SR.Spaced repetition queue lives in on-device SQLite. Server is the catch-up sync, not the source of truth.
AI is a worker, not a dependency.Strip the enrich service and the app still runs. Hooks just stay raw.
Community in plain text.YAML cards in /content. Anyone can grep, fork, PR.
Ship the share image.SSR-rendered Open Graph at /card/[id]/opengraph-image. Free distribution.

08What I'd build first

In this order, two-week sprints:

  1. Schema + ingest. Get 1k Tatoeba sentences and 200 Wikipedia leads into Postgres. No app yet.
  2. API skeleton. /v1/feed with the simplest mix rule and a hand-tuned ranker. No auth, no AI.
  3. Web client. Wire the prototype you've already approved to the real API.
  4. Auth + progress sync. Magic link, cookie session, FSRS state.
  5. Enrich worker. Hook rewriter first — it's the highest-leverage AI job. Measure dwell time on rewritten vs raw.
  6. Mobile (Expo). Reuse packages/ui. Native gesture polish.
  7. Share image SSR. Once 100 users care.
  8. Community submissions. When users start asking "how do I add a card?"
learnscroll · architecture v0← back to app
